findings.short_text: The active dataset contains 1,000 objects, with 280 PHA labels and 720 non-PHA labels.
findings.technical_basis: Counts are computed from the active gold/risk-score table.
findings.title: The current observatory dataset is analyzable

findings.caveat: Sentry fields are sparse and should not be interpreted as complete alerting.
findings.short_text: 2 of 1,000 objects carry a Sentry signal in the current dataset.
findings.technical_basis: Sentry count is derived from the sentry_flag field.
findings.title: Sentry signal is limited to a small subset

findings.short_text: The largest risk category is moderate; 909 objects fall there.
findings.technical_basis: Risk categories are counted from the current risk_scores table.
findings.title: Most objects concentrate in moderate priority

findings.short_text: 21 objects are currently elevated or above, keeping the highest-priority review list compact.
findings.technical_basis: Elevated objects are risk_category in elevated, high or critical.
findings.title: Elevated priority objects are a focused review set

findings.short_text: Among the highest-ranked objects, physical size and orbital proximity are the dominant components.
findings.technical_basis: Dominant components are counted across the top 20 rows by risk_score_0_100.
findings.title: Top-ranked objects are driven by physical and orbital factors

findings.short_text: 14 of 21 simulated objects have category-shift probability at or below 20%.
findings.technical_basis: Category-shift probability is read from score Monte Carlo results.
findings.title: Most simulated top objects retain their priority band

findings.short_text: The highest shift-probability objects should be inspected before treating their category as stable.
findings.title: Some objects show score sensitivity near category thresholds

findings.short_text: 50 objects have saved orbital perturbation results.
findings.title: Approximate orbital scenarios are available for priority objects

findings.short_text: Objects with the highest dispersion index deserve closer review in the orbital simulation view.
findings.title: Orbital uncertainty varies across the priority set

findings.short_text: The graph contains 1,000 nodes and 6,955 similarity edges.
findings.technical_basis: Nodes and edges are counted from exported graph Parquet files.
findings.title: The orbital graph forms an analytical neighborhood

findings.short_text: Nearest-neighbor links can be used to inspect orbitally similar objects around a selected NEO.
findings.technical_basis: Edges are kNN orbital-similarity links built from scaled orbital and risk-context features.
findings.title: Graph neighborhoods support object-level comparison

findings.short_text: random_forest on orbital_only is the current defensible model-evidence view.
findings.technical_basis: The evidence builder excludes definition-heavy feature sets from the best defensible pick.
findings.title: Best defensible evidence avoids definition-heavy leakage

findings.short_text: 104 object/model rows are flagged in disagreement outputs.
findings.technical_basis: Disagreements compare high-confidence predictions against observed labels or model families.
findings.title: Model disagreements create an inspection queue
